name: safeshell
summary: 🛡️ SafeShell: A security tool for shell history management, designed to detect, stash, and clear sensitive commands, ensuring safer terminal sessions.

documentation.readme: 🛡️ SafeShell: A security tool for shell history management, designed to detect, stash, and clear sensitive commands, ensuring safer terminal sessions.

use_cases: When you need Penetration Testing, use safeshell because 🛡️ SafeShell: A security tool for shell history management, designed to detect, stash, and clear sensitive commands, ensuring safer terminal sessions.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `___) || (_| ||  _||  __/ ___) || | | ||  __/| || |` as documented upstream for safeshell.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cargo install --path .` as documented upstream for safeshell.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `curl -sS $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL | bash` as documented upstream for safeshell.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `iwr $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL -useb | iex` as documented upstream for safeshell.
When chaining Penetration Testing tools, pair safeshell with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `safeshell --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
